data.title: S&P 500 looks expensive, but for good reason, BofA's Subramanian says
data.description:
The Standard & Poor’s 500 (SP500) stock index is statistically expensive on every valuation metric tracked by Bank of America, but equity and quant strategist Savita Subramanian argues this is a feature, not a bug, of today's high-quality, tech-heavy index.

In a June 12 report to clients, Subramanian said the S&P 500 (SP500) trades at 21 times forward earnings, about 35% above its historical average, and looks overpriced across all 20 valuation metrics BofA monitors. Yet she cautions against historical comparisons that ignore how dramatically the index has evolved.

Nearly 70% of the S&P in 1980 was made up of asset-intensive manufacturing sectors, the report said, adding that today the figure is less than 20%. The valuation conversation is a comparison of “apples and oranges,” Subramanian said.

Today’s index boasts higher quality, lower leverage, reduced earnings volatility and stronger margins. These structural improvements make mean reversion to historical valuation norms a misleading expectation, she argues.

U.S. premium to global markets is justified

Subramanian also defends the approximately 40% valuation premium the U.S. commands over Europe and Asia, pointing to superior balance sheets, corporate transparency, and long-term growth potential.

According to BofA’s research:

  • The U.S. is half as levered as other global regions
  • Earnings volatility is lower than in Europe
  • Free cash flow per share is higher than both Asia and Europe
  • The percentage of non-earning companies is lower
  • Forecast dispersion is tighter, reflecting greater transparency

Add in structural advantages such as the U.S. dollar’s reserve currency status, energy independence and dominance in technology, and Subramanian sees little reason for the valuation gap to close.

Sector strategy: Positioning for summer

BofA’s tactical model suggests investors lean into Communication Services, Utilities and Technology within the U.S., identifying Interactive Media & Services, Metals & Mining and Independent Power & Renewable Electricity Producers as top “opportunity” industries.

Outside the U.S., Subramanian recommends being selective:

  • Favor U.S. Utilities over Asia Utilities
  • Choose Asia Communication Services over European peers
  • Consider European Consumer Discretionary over U.S. names

Meanwhile, Consumer Staples Distribution & Retail stands out as a potential “momentum blowup,” a warning for investors chasing recent winners without underlying support.

data.title: Top weekly S&P 500 consumer discretionary gainers & losers: Tesla takes lead; Lululemon bottoms
data.description:
For the week ending June 13, the S&P 500 Consumer Discretionary sector (NYSEARCA:XLY) saw a decline in price of 1.00%, underperforming the broader S&P 500's (SP500) 0.48% decline. However, the sector's year-to-date performance is even weaker, down 5.62%, significantly trailing the benchmark's 1.62% rise.

Global markets faced a fresh wave of uncertainty this week, impacting consumer discretionary stocks, after Israel's military strike on Iran escalated Middle East tensions. This added pressure to markets already strained by President Trump's aggressive and unpredictable trade policies.

Top S&P 500 consumer discretionary gainers:

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) +10.22% as President Trump and CEO Elon Musk mend their relationship. Speaking at the White House earlier on Thursday, Trump called Musk “a friend” and has reopened dialogue with the Tesla CEO after a very public feud last week.

Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) +4.04% It is reducing prices on some iced drinks in China to stay competitive amid cautious consumers. CEO Brian Niccol noted strong interest in selling a stake in its China business. The company is also testing a new protein cold foam at select U. S. locations to improve its menu.

General Motors’ (NYSE:GM) +2.49% due to plans for a plug-in hybrid, increased U.S. investments, and an optimistic Q2 earnings forecast, according to GM's chief financial officer Paul Jacobson at the Deutsche Bank Global Auto Industry Conference.

Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) +2.25%

D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI) +1.94%

Top S&P 500 consumer discretionary losers:

lululemon athletica (NASDAQ:LULU) -9.86% after Barclays reduced LULU's price target to $270 from $276, maintaining an Equal Weight rating. The company's Q1 results met expectations, but same-store sales growth fell short of estimates due to traffic issues, the analyst tells investors in a research note.

Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) -9.10%

Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST) -8.31%

Carnival Corporation (CCL) -7.70%

Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK) -7.09%

Notable Consumer Discretionary ETFs include: (NYSEARCA:XLY), (NYSEARCA:VCR), (NYSEARCA:FXD), (NYSEARCA:FDIS), (NYSEARCA:RSPD), and (NYSEARCA:RXI).

data.title: Oil spike amplifies the current stagflation shock - Apollo
data.description:
The jump in oil prices following Israel's strikes on Iran could exacerbate U.S. stagflationary conditions if they persist, according to Apollo Global Management.

Oil prices (CL1:COM) (CO1:COM) (NYSEARCA:USO) (BNO) rallied more than 7% in Friday's session, the biggest one-day rise in three years.

"According to the Fed’s model of the US economy, a sustained $10 increase in oil prices is expected to increase inflation by 0.4% (including secondary effects) and lower GDP by 0.4%," Chief Economist Torsten Slock said.

WTI is up more than $10 from its lows at the end of May.

"Tariffs also increase inflation and lower GDP growth," he said. "Restrictions on immigration also increase wage inflation and lower employment growth."

"In short, higher oil prices exacerbate the ongoing stagflation shock stemming from tariffs and immigration restrictions."

"Stagflation is a problem for the FOMC when they meet," Slock added. "Higher inflation says the Fed should be hiking. Lower GDP growth says the Fed should cutting. So will the FOMC next week put more weight on the upward pressure on inflation or more weight on the coming slowdown in growth?"

data.title: Weekly top industrial gainers and losers: Northrop Grumman leads, United Airlines lags amid Israel-Iran tensions
data.description:
Wall Street reacted sharply last week after geopolitical tensions flared following Israel’s attack on Iran’s nuclear facilities.

Overall, it was a volatile week for the industrial sector, with travel stocks hit hardest amid heightened tensions in the Middle East. Planemaker Boeing (NYSE:BA) and its suppliers also came under pressure after a deadly crash involving one of its models in India, claimed the lives of all but one passenger.

The Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLI) declined 0.65% compared to a 0.66% rise in the S&P500 index.

Weekly top 5 gainers

  • Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) ended as the highest gainer last week with its shares +5.58% after BMO Capital Markets included the company in its list of industrial stocks that fit with the growth-at-a-reasonable-price (GARP) characteristics.
  • L3Harris Technologies (NYSE:LHX) was +5.21% in the week after Seeking Alpha analyst  Narek Hovhannisyan issued a Buy call for the stock on the back of a balanced risk-return profile and solid business model.
  • RTX (NYSE:RTX) ended +4.74% after Raytheon was  awarded a $646M contract to continue producing AN/SPY-6(V) radars for the U.S. Navy.
  • Huntington Ingalls (NYSE:HII) and Textron (NYSE:TXT) shares ended +2.76% and +2.40% respectively as both the companies also made it to BMO Capital Markets’ list of industrial stocks fitting GARP characteristics.

Weekly top 5 losers

  • Travel-related stocks bore the brunt of heightened Israel-Iran tensions last week. United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) emerged as the biggest loser with its shares -12.15% in the week.
  • Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) was second on the list with its shares -7.67%.
  • GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE), a supplier for Boeing (NYSE:BA), was -7.44% last week after one of the latter’s 787 Dreamliners crashed in India.
  • Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) ended the week -5.23%.
  • Boeing (BA) was -4.97% after the crash of one of its 787 Dreamliners in India raised fresh questions about the safety of its planes. Reports suggest that the Indian government will likely  ground the Dreamliner 787-8 fleet for a safety review. 

ETFs to tab the industrial sector include: (NYSEARCA:XLI), (VIS), (XAR), (EXI), (PSCI), (PRN), (FIDU).

data.title: Boeing facility in Kyiv is said to be damaged in Russian airstrike
data.description:
A Boeing (NYSE:BA) facility in Ukraine's capital was reportedly hit during a recent wave of Russian aerial assaults, the Financial Times reported, citing six sources familiar with the incident as well as photographs reviewed by the publication.

The strike occurred during a large-scale attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian regions early last week, involving over 300 drones and several missiles, according to a statement by Ukraine’s Air Force on Tuesday.

Among the damaged sites was a building used by Boeing (NYSE:BA) in Kyiv, the Financial Times reported, referencing two company employees, three Ukrainian officials, and the head of the American Chamber of Commerce in Ukraine.

Despite the damage, Boeing’s (BA) operations in Ukraine have not been disrupted, said Andriy Koryagin, deputy general director for the company’s Ukrainian operations. He noted that all staff remained safe during the assault.

A Boeing (BA) spokesperson declined to provide further details to the Financial Times but emphasized that employee safety remains the company’s top priority. No injuries were reported.

Boeing (BA) currently employs more than 1,000 people throughout Ukraine, according to the report.

Reuters was unable to independently verify the incident, and requests for comment from Boeing (BA) and the American Chamber of Commerce in Ukraine had not been answered at the time of publication.

data.title: Taiwan adds Huawei, SMIC to export control list amid escalating tensions
data.description:
Taiwan’s Ministry of Economic Affairs placed Chinese tech giants Huawei Technologies and Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp. (OTCQX:SIUIF) on its list of restricted export recipients, signaling deepening trade and technology rifts involving Taipei, Beijing and Washington, The Associated Press reported.

The updated list, revised on Sunday, includes entities considered part of the "strategic high-tech commodities" category. Taiwanese firms must now apply for special licenses before exporting certain goods to the newly added companies. Others on the list include designated groups such as al-Qaeda and the Taliban, along with a range of organizations and firms from China, Iran and additional regions.

Huawei and SMIC, both previously sanctioned by the United States, have yet to issue public statements regarding their addition to the Taiwanese export control registry.

Both companies play key roles in China’s effort to develop domestic alternatives to U.S. semiconductor technology. In particular, they are producing China’s most advanced AI chips, aiming to challenge market leader Nvidia and support local tech firms struggling with ongoing U.S. export restrictions.

Taiwan itself is a global semiconductor powerhouse, home to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (NYSE:TSM) (OTC:TSMWF), the world’s biggest contract chipmaker and a major supplier to Nvidia. In late 2023, the U.S. instructed TSMC to stop delivering certain high-end chips to Chinese firms, further tightening restrictions on China’s access to cutting-edge semiconductors.

The developments come amid ongoing geopolitical strain. China continues to assert its claim over self-governed Taiwan, while the United States remains the island’s most important informal partner and its leading supplier of defense equipment.

data.title: Trump posts he will bring peace 'soon' between Israel and Iran
data.description:
President Donald Trump said Sunday there will be peace between Israel and Iran soon as he shepherds a deal.

"Iran and Israel should make a deal, and will make a deal, just like I got India and Pakistan to make, in that case by using TRADE with the United States to bring reason, cohesion, and sanity into the talks with two excellent leaders who were able to quickly make a decision and STOP!" Trump posted on Truth Social.

"Likewise, we will have PEACE, soon, between Israel and Iran! Many calls and meetings now taking place," he said.

data.title: Tel Aviv stocks rebound as markets gauge effects of Iran-Israel conflict
data.description:
Israeli equities closed higher on Sunday despite an escalation in hostilities between Israel and Iran, with investors appearing to shake off early concerns about the regional conflict. The Tel Aviv Stock Exchange’s benchmark TA-35 index ended the day up 1%, recovering from morning losses that had approached 2%.

Sunday marked the first trading session since a dramatic exchange of missile strikes between the two nations began on Friday. Market sentiment wavered early in the day, weighed down by fresh violence and a steep selloff on Wall Street late last week. But by the close, equities had broadly regained ground.

Ronen Menachem, chief markets economist at Mizrahi Tefahot Bank, was quoted by Reuters as saying that investor reactions were initially driven by geopolitical shock and global market weakness. Going forward, investor sentiment is likely to be influenced by how the conflict unfolds, particularly its scope, duration and how the United States chooses to respond, he said.

While local government bonds saw slight gains, trading in the Israeli shekel was paused for the day. The currency had weakened on Friday, falling from 3.5 to 3.61 per dollar.

Iran retaliates to Israel’s strikes

Israel reported that its military had launched targeted strikes on Iranian facilities tied to nuclear development and missile production over the weekend. Iran retaliated with a barrage of rockets, marking the second consecutive day of direct conflict. At least 10 Israeli civilians were killed overnight, including three children, and more than 140 were injured.

In response to the attacks, the Home Front Command implemented emergency measures, including a ban on public gatherings, remote learning for schools and a shift to remote work for non-essential employees. Despite this, the Bank of Israel announced that Sunday would proceed as a standard business day. Major bank branches remained open and central authorities emphasized that financial systems and payment infrastructure were functioning normally.

“The Bank has all necessary tools to support economic continuity,” the central bank said in a statement.

A senior Finance Ministry official told Reuters that while the conflict may result in increased spending, Israel enters this period from a position of financial strength, with deep liquidity in domestic markets. The official also said the heightened military activity could prompt credit rating agencies to reassess Israel’s standing, though the shekel’s performance suggests there is no sense of crisis for now.

Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich said preparations had been underway for weeks and that the government was ready to act as needed, Reuters reported.

data.title: Trump reports over $57M in crypto earnings, along with income from resorts
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump disclosed $57.7 million in income from cryptocurrency token sales linked to a firm he and his sons co-founded, according to federal financial disclosure documents cited by multiple news outlets. The crypto earnings make up part of the more than $600 million in income reported by the president.

The filing, published Friday by the Office of Government Ethics, offers a comprehensive snapshot of Trump’s finances, revealing earnings across his business empire. His holdings include everything from real estate and hospitality ventures to digital assets and crypto projects.

The crypto windfall stems from World Liberty Financial, a blockchain-based venture launched ahead of the 2024 election. Trump and his sons Donald Jr., Eric and Barron are listed as co-founders on the company’s website.

Trump’s biggest source of private income came from Trump Endeavor 12 Llc, a Miami-based entity that owns several golf properties and a resort, which brought in $110 million. Meanwhile, his Mar-a-Lago estate generated more than $50 million in revenue last year.

The president, whose net worth is estimated at $4.8 billion by the Bloomberg Billionaires Index, reported 22 assets with individual values exceeding $50 million, including Mar-a-Lago, his golf resort in Turnberry, Scotland, and shares in World Liberty Financial and Trump Media & Technology Group, the parent company of Truth Social.

Because the disclosure form uses broad valuation ranges (with “over $50 million” being the highest), it doesn’t provide precise figures for calculating net worth. Trump Media is currently valued at approximately $2.2 billion.

Meme coin assets

A separate crypto-related company, Fight Fight Fight Llc, the operator behind Trump’s meme coin, launched in January 2025 and wasn't covered in the 2024 disclosure. However, in May, it hosted an exclusive dinner for top holders of the $TRUMP token, which saw its value spike by 56% after the event was announced.

Another digital asset stream, CIC Digital Llc, which licenses Trump’s image for NFTs, generated $1.1 million in income last year and holds an Ethereum wallet valued at more than $1 million.

The 234-page filing also catalogues Trump's portfolio of trademarks across countries such as China, Taiwan, South Korea and Venezuela. It includes personal investments and holdings of First Lady Melania Trump.

Trump reported 11 unresolved debts, including two related to civil judgments awarded to author E. Jean Carroll and one stemming from his felony conviction in a criminal fraud case. These obligations are on hold while appeals are pending. Legal bills tied to his various lawsuits were not listed, as they have largely been covered by his Save America PAC.

The disclosure also reveals Trump holds seven real estate loans, including mortgages of more than $50 million each on Trump Tower, Trump National Doral and 40 Wall Street. He also reported at least $15,000 in credit card debt on his American Express account.

Separately, Vice President J.D. Vance disclosed that his household, including wife Usha Vance, held assets worth at least $6.5 million, Bloomberg News reported.

data.title: Notable healthcare headlines for the week: Novo Nordisk, Thermo Fisher and UnitedHealth in focus
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages plummeted on Friday after Israel and Iran traded missile strikes and stoked concerns of an all-out war in the Middle East.

Iran retaliated on Friday with its own projectiles after Israel’s attack on some of Iran’s nuclear sites and military targets on late Thursday.

Week-to-date, the S&P (SP500) lost 0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was down 0.6%, and the Dow (DJI) declined 1.3%.

However, the S&P 500 Health Care Index Sector (NYSEARCA:XLV) gained nearly 2% during the week.

The top S&P 500 healthcare gainers and losers for the last week are as follows:

Top Gainers:

·       Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) +7.30%

·       Eli Lilly (LLY) +6.43%

·       CVS Health (CVS) +6.10%

·       Pfizer (PFE) +5.10%

·       Cardinal Health (CAH) +4.33%

Top Losers:

·       Universal Health Services (UHS) -9.67%

·       Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) -8.06%

·       DexCom (DXCM) -5.40%

·       The Cooper Companies (COO) -3.68%

·       Zoetis (ZTS) -3.43%

Here are some of the important healthcare stories from this week:

Novo Nordisk rises on report activist Parvus building stake

Novo Nordisk (NVO) shares hit their highest level in over two months following a Financial Times report that activist hedge fund Parvus Asset Management is building a stake in the Danish drugmaker. The stock rose as much as 2.5% in early trading in Copenhagen. Novo Nordisk told Seeking Alpha in an email that it does not have anything to “share on this topic.” The report comes as the pharma giant faces growing challenges in maintaining its dominance in the obesity drug market.

In other news of note, Novo Nordisk (NVO) has initiated a new Phase 3 trial for its next-gen weight loss therapy CagriSema after the Danish drugmaker's REDEFINE 1 Phase 3 trial for the treatment disappointed Wall Street late last year.

Additionally, Nvidia (NVDA) announced a collaboration with Novo Nordisk (NVO) on Wednesday to speed up the Danish pharma giant's efforts in drug discovery using artificial intelligence.

Thermo Fisher said to weigh sale of some diagnostic assets for $4B

Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) is looking to sell some of its lower-growth diagnostic assets for around $4B, the Financial Times reported, citing three people familiar with the matter.

The company's advisers approached private equity firms in recent weeks to gauge interest in a possible deal. However, there is no guarantee that a deal will materialize.

The diagnostics business, which includes Thermo Fisher's (TMO) microbiology unit that makes infectious disease testing solutions, generates over $1B in annual sales.

UnitedHealth weighs $1B deal for Banmedica in bid to exit Latin America - report

UnitedHealth (UNH) is considering four non-binding bids for its Banmedica unit for about $1B, two people with direct knowledge of the matter told Reuters, as the largest U.S. health insurer doubles down on efforts to exit Latin America.

The company is seeking $1B for Banmedica, which operates in Colombia and Chile, and expects to fix a deadline for binding offers next month. It'd acquired Banmedica for $2.8B in 2018.

UnitedHealth (UNH) reportedly received bids from U.S. private equity firm Acon Investments, Latin America-focused asset manager Patria Investments (PAX), Texas-based non-profit Christus Health, and Latin American healthcare firm Auna (AUNA).

While UnitedHealth (UNH) has been trying to exit Latin America since 2022 amid mounting losses, efforts have ramped up in recent months in the wake of its CEO's abrupt exit and an ongoing criminal probe.

BioNTech to buy German rival CureVac for $1.25B in all-stock deal

BioNTech (BNTX) has agreed to acquire its German rival CureVac (CVAC) in an all-stock transaction worth nearly $1.25B, the companies announced Thursday. Shares of CureVac (CVAC) jumped ~26% in reaction to the announcement, while BioNTech (BNTX) ADRs traded flat.

Per the terms of the deal, each CureVac (CVAC) share will be exchanged for approx. $5.46 in BioNTech American Depositary Shares (“ADSs”), indicating a premium of 55% to the former’s volume-weighted average price over the past three months.

CVAC shareholders are expected to own 4%–6% of BioNTech (BNTX) after the transaction, which gives the company an implied equity value of ~$1.25B.

Drugmakers complain clarity lacking from administration on lowering drug prices

Pharmaceutical executives say that the Trump administration has not provided enough details as to how it will achieve the lowered drug prices promised in an Executive Order issued in May.

That order implements a program known as "Most Favored Nation" that ties the prices of prescription medicines in the U.S. to the lowest prices found abroad.
